title: White China Plates II
content:
You could have driven
        a pick-up truck
        thru spokes of that moon, so big and radiant
        this upended water chestnut - 
        ground mist weeping
        in the shadows
        flutter of an old woman's shawl,
        the clammy smell like
        a child's fingers to the face,
        a little unsettling
        crickets and dew in brigades
        running tears on the old
        shoe leather.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Cameron Brown, titled "White China Plates II", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
